tickets.title: Implement calculator module with scientific operations
tickets.description:
Create app/utils/calculator.py with six scientific calculator functions:

1. multiply(a, b) - multiply two numbers
2. divide(a, b) - divide with ZeroDivisionError handling when b is 0
3. power(base, exp) - raise base to exponent power
4. sqrt(n) - square root with ValueError for negative numbers
5. modulo(a, b) - remainder operation
6. factorial(n) - factorial for non-negative integers (raise ValueError for negative)

All functions must:
- Include proper type hints (use float | int for numeric types)
- Have comprehensive docstrings following Google/NumPy style
- Handle edge cases appropriately (division by zero, negative sqrt, negative factorial)
- Export functions in app/utils/__init__.py

Acceptance criteria:
- File app/utils/calculator.py exists with all 6 functions
- All functions have type hints and docstrings
- Functions raise appropriate exceptions for invalid inputs
- Functions are exported in app/utils/__init__.py
tickets.priority_rationale: Core feature implementation - required foundation for calculator functionality
verification: python -c 'from app.utils import multiply, divide, power, sqrt, modulo, factorial; assert multiply(2, 3) == 6'
python -c 'from app.utils.calculator import divide; import pytest; pytest.raises(ZeroDivisionError, divide, 1, 0)'
tickets.notes: Use math.sqrt and math.factorial from standard library. For modulo, use % operator. Ensure type hints support both int and float inputs.

tickets.title: Add comprehensive test coverage for calculator module
tickets.description:
Create tests/test_calculator.py with comprehensive test coverage for all calculator functions.

Test requirements:
- Test normal operation for each function with various inputs
- Test error cases: ZeroDivisionError for divide(1, 0), ValueError for sqrt(-1) and factorial(-1)
- Test edge cases: factorial(0) = 1, power(2, 0) = 1, modulo with negative numbers
- Test type handling: ensure functions work with both int and float inputs
- Use pytest fixtures where appropriate
- Follow existing test patterns from tests/test_middleware.py

Acceptance criteria:
- File tests/test_calculator.py exists
- All 6 functions have test coverage
- Error cases are tested and raise correct exceptions
- Edge cases are covered
- Tests pass with pytest
tickets.priority_rationale: Critical for ensuring code quality and preventing regressions - tests validate correctness of core functionality
tickets.notes: Use pytest.raises context manager for exception testing. Consider parametrize for testing multiple input combinations.

tickets.title: Update README.md to document calculator module
tickets.description:
Create or update README.md in project root to document the calculator module and its scientific operations.

Documentation should include:
- Overview of the calculator module
- List of available functions with brief descriptions
- Usage examples for each function
- Error handling information (what exceptions are raised and when)
- Installation/setup instructions if needed
- Link to test file for examples

Acceptance criteria:
- README.md exists in project root
- Calculator module is documented with all 6 functions
- Usage examples are provided
- Error handling is documented
- Documentation is clear and follows project conventions
tickets.priority_rationale: Important for usability and maintainability, but not blocking for functionality - documentation helps users understand the module
tickets.notes: If README.md doesn't exist, create a new one. If it exists, add a new section for the calculator module. Include code examples using triple backticks.
